question: A shopkeeper buys an article for ₹800 and sells it for ₹920. What is the profit percentage?
The ratio of two numbers is 3:5. If the sum of the numbers is 160, what is the larger number?
A can complete a work in 12 days and B can complete the same work in 18 days. In how many days will they complete the work together?
A train travels 360 km in 4 hours. What is its speed in m/s?
The average of five numbers is 42. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 38. What is the excluded number?
A person invests ₹5000 at 10% simple interest per annum. What is the total amount after 3 years?
If 30% of a number is 45, what is 50% of that number?
What comes next in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
A and B together can complete a task in 8 days. A alone can do it in 12 days. How many days does B alone take?
Two trains are moving in opposite directions at 60 km/h and 90 km/h. Their lengths are 120 m and 180 m respectively. How long will they take to cross each other?
What is the compound interest on ₹10,000 at 10% per annum for 2 years, compounded annually?
A merchant marks his goods 40% above cost price and then offers a 20% discount. What is his profit percentage?
Find the missing number in the pattern: 3, 9, 27, 81, ?
Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the son of my father's only daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?
If APPLE is coded as ELPPA, how is MANGO coded?
A man walks 5 km North, then turns right and walks 3 km, then turns right again and walks 5 km. In which direction is he from his starting point?
All roses are flowers. Some flowers are red. Which of the following conclusions is definitely true?
In a row of 30 students, Rakesh is 12th from the left and Suresh is 17th from the right. How many students are between them?
Book is to Reading as Fork is to ___?
If 'P + Q' means 'P is the father of Q', 'P - Q' means 'P is the mother of Q', and 'P × Q' means 'P is the brother of Q', what does 'A + B × C' mean?
options: A is the father of C's brother
A is the uncle of C
B is the brother of C and A is the father of B
A is the grandfather of C

question: Which number replaces the question mark? 7, 14, 28, 56, ?
Five people A, B, C, D, E are sitting in a row facing north. C is to the immediate right of A. B is at the extreme left. D is between B and A. Who is at the extreme right?
If JOURNAL is coded as KPVSOBM, how is TORTURE coded?
Statement: All cats are dogs. All dogs are animals. Conclusion I: All cats are animals. Conclusion II: All animals are cats. Which conclusion(s) follow?
Choose the correctly punctuated sentence:
options: Its a beautiful day isnt it.
It's a beautiful day, isn't it?
Its a beautiful day, isnt it?
It's a beautiful day isnt it.

question: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant':
Choose the antonym of 'Benevolent':
Fill in the blank: 'She is so dedicated ___ her work that she rarely takes a day off.'
Identify the grammatically correct sentence:
options: Each of the students have submitted their assignment.
Each of the students has submitted his or her assignment.
Each of the students have submitted his or her assignment.
Each of the student has submitted their assignment.

question: What does the idiom 'Break the ice' mean?
options: To initiate a conversation in a social setting

question: Choose the word that best completes the sentence: 'The manager's decision was ___; no one could change his mind.'
Choose the synonym of 'Pragmatic':
Fill in the blank: 'Neither the manager ___ the employees were present at the meeting.'
What does the idiom 'Burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work late into the night

question:
The following table shows the monthly sales (in units) of a company:

Month   | Product A | Product B | Product C
Jan     | 120       | 200       | 150
Feb     | 150       | 180       | 170
Mar     | 180       | 220       | 160
Apr     | 200       | 190       | 180

What is the total sales of Product B across all four months?
Using the table from Q35: In which month was the combined sales of all three products the highest?

A pie chart shows the distribution of a company's expenses: Salaries 40%, Rent 20%, Marketing 15%, Utilities 10%, Miscellaneous 15%. If the total expenses are ₹5,00,000, how much is spent on Marketing?

The following data shows the number of employees in different departments:

Department | 2023 | 2024
IT         | 50   | 65
HR         | 30   | 36
Sales      | 45   | 54
Finance    | 25   | 30

Which department had the highest percentage increase in employees from 2023 to 2024?
A bar chart shows the quarterly revenue (in lakhs) of a firm: Q1 = 40, Q2 = 55, Q3 = 65, Q4 = 80. What is the percentage increase in revenue from Q1 to Q4?
Using the employee data from Q38: What is the total number of employees across all departments in 2024?
